title: MySQL
objectives: Choose isolation levels and lock modes

sections.markdown: MySQL is the world's most-deployed open-source relational database. Its default InnoDB engine offers ACID transactions, row-level locking, foreign keys, and a **clustered index** — rows are physically stored in primary-key order.

sections.code:
SELECT id, name FROM users
WHERE email = 'a@x.com'    -- must have an index on email
FOR UPDATE;                -- row lock for concurrent-safe update

-- InnoDB: PK = clustered index; secondary indexes store the PK, not row ptr

sections.markdown: InnoDB clustered index means: choose your PK carefully — a UUID PK causes random inserts (page splits, bloat). Prefer AUTO_INCREMENT or a time-ordered UUID (UUIDv7/ULID) for hot tables.
Believing 'MySQL is not ACID.' InnoDB has been ACID for over 20 years. Just don't use MyISAM in 2026 — it has no transactions and no crash safety.
Use InnoDB, UTF8MB4 charset (real 4-byte Unicode/emoji), covering indexes for hot queries, connection pooling (HikariCP), and async logical replication for read replicas.

flashcards.back: Rows are physically stored in primary-key order; secondary indexes store the PK, not a row pointer.
REPEATABLE READ, via MVCC snapshots — prevents non-repeatable reads without locking readers.

flashcards.front: Why avoid random UUIDs as PK?
flashcards.back: They cause random inserts into the clustered index, leading to page splits and index bloat.

quiz.explain: Clustered index = physical row layout by PK.
REPEATABLE READ via MVCC — snapshot per transaction.

quiz.q: Best PK for a high-insert table?
quiz.explain: Time-ordered PKs keep inserts append-only in the clustered index.

challenges.text: Create a users table with a covering index and prove it via EXPLAIN.
Demonstrate REPEATABLE READ preventing a non-repeatable read.
Reproduce a deadlock; fix by ordering lock acquisition.
Set up primary + read replica; route reads via a proxy (ProxySQL/Vitess).

interviewQuestions: Explain isolation levels; which is MySQL's default?
Why avoid random UUIDs as PK?
How does replication work in MySQL?
